search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae telehealth type 2 diabetes monitoring glucose review 2024
site:diabetesjournals.org care 2026 diabetes technology blood glucose monitoring reviewed data
site:diabetesjournals.org care 2026 comprehensive diabetes evaluation monitoring hypoglycaemia
candidate_sources_rejected: search snippets that assign glycaemic targets or interpret patterns
pregnancy and paediatric diabetes guidance applied to an unspecified population
rejection_reasons: Manufacturer material cannot support clinical interpretation or treatment.
The workflow contains no entered readings, diabetes type, medicine regimen, pregnancy, age or acute metabolic context.
No source permits automatic glucose interpretation, target assignment, diagnosis, urgency or treatment change.
population_applicability: People with clinician-established diabetes reviewing entered capillary or CGM records; suspected diabetes, pregnancy, gestational diabetes, children, frailty, renal failure, steroid exposure and acute metabolic illness require separately qualified evidence.
setting_applicability: DHA telehealth and clinician-led diabetes follow-up evidence is partially applicable to a general GP documentation scaffold; device data must be entered and interpreted by the clinician.
UAE_applicability: A current DHA telehealth source supplies direct UAE context for established type 2 diabetes, while ADA sections supplement documentation domains only; local UAE laboratory, device, medicine, referral and emergency pathways govern use.
recency_verification: DHA issue 2 (2024) and official ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 articles were verified current on 2026-07-15.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue or newer ADA annual standards were identified on the official pages reviewed on 2026-07-15.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to established type 2 diabetes follow-up context, symptoms, complications and clinician review; it does not validate a generic glucose log or infer control.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant only to clinician-recorded concern features in an established diabetes review and cannot classify an entered reading or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to clinician-led monitoring documentation but does not prescribe frequency or interpret values in this scaffold.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to recording already available laboratory context only; it does not generate testing or results.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to recording monitoring method, preferences, training and data-sharing context without selecting a device.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to clinician review of entered blood-glucose records, method and accuracy concerns without interpreting readings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to documenting CGM use, reviewed data and device concerns without calculating or interpreting CGM metrics.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Relevant to established-diabetes follow-up domains and already available monitoring or laboratory context, not diagnosis or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded initial, follow-up and annual medical history, medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring, blood pressure, weight, examination, laboratory and complication-screening context.

unresolved_source_gaps: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
No readings, dates, meal context, symptoms, device, diabetes type, treatment, pregnancy, age, ketones, laboratory values or clinician interpretation are present.
No automatic diagnosis, calculation, target, urgency, monitoring frequency, medicine change, dietary advice or referral is supported.
